---
Can CNNs Trained on DL-Inferred KL Pseudo-Labels from MRKR Classify Knee OA Severity and Generalise to Radiologist-Labelled OAI/Kaggle Radiographs, Particularly for Early OA Grades?

---

**Abstract** (~300 words)
- Background
- Methods
- Results (placeholder)
- Conclusions (placeholder)

---

**1. Introduction** (~400 words)
- 1.1 Clinical burden of knee osteoarthritis
- 1.2 Kellgren-Lawrence grading system and its limitations
- 1.3 Deep learning for automated KL grading — state of the art
- 1.4 Gap in literature — DL-inferred labels, fairness analysis
- 1.5 Research objectives and study overview

---

**2. Methods** (~700 words)

- 2.1 Study design and reporting framework
    - Retrospective diagnostic performance evaluation
    - TRIPOD+AI reporting
    - No ethics approval required — anonymised public data

- 2.2 Datasets
    - 2.2.1 Primary training dataset — MRKR
        - Dataset description and access
        - Label source — DL-inferred KL grades
        - Demographics — sex, race, ethnicity
    - 2.2.2 External validation dataset — OAI/Kaggle
        - Dataset description
        - Label source — radiologist graded
        - Rationale for cross-dataset validation

- 2.3 Data preparation
    - 2.3.1 Inclusion criteria and filter cascade
        - Frontal views, no arthroplasty, valid KL grades
        - Bilateral expansion — one row per knee
        - Stratified random sample — 2,000 per grade
    - 2.3.2 Preprocessing pipeline
        - Bilateral knee separation — rule-based crop with horizontal_flip correction
        - CLAHE contrast enhancement — clip_limit=2.0, tile_grid=8×8
    - 2.3.3 Train/validation/test split
        - Patient-level stratified 70/15/15
        - Data leakage prevention

- 2.4 Model architectures
    - 2.4.1 ResNet50
    - 2.4.2 DenseNet121
    - 2.4.3 EfficientNet-B0
    - Transfer learning and fine-tuning rationale

- 2.5 Training configuration
    - 2.5.1 Ordinal loss function
        - Formula and justification
        - Comparison to standard cross-entropy
    - 2.5.2 Optimisation
        - Adam optimiser, lr=1e-4
        - ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler
        - Early stopping
    - 2.5.3 Augmentation
        - RandomHorizontalFlip, RandomRotation, ColorJitter
        - RandomResizedCrop exclusion — clinical justification
    - 2.5.4 Architecture-specific image sizes
    - 2.5.5 Reproducibility — three seeds (42, 123, 456)

- 2.6 Evaluation strategy
    - 2.6.1 Primary metrics
        - Balanced accuracy, Macro F1, MAE, Grade-1 recall
    - 2.6.2 Stability analysis across seeds
    - 2.6.3 External validation — OAI/Kaggle
    - 2.6.4 Subgroup fairness analysis
        - Sex, race, ethnicity, weightbearing, age group, laterality

---

**3. Results** (~600 words + tables/figures)

- 3.1 Dataset characteristics
    - Filter cascade summary table
    - Demographics breakdown table
    - Weightbearing and acquisition type distribution

- 3.2 Model comparison — MRKR test set
    - Table: Balanced accuracy / Macro F1 / MAE / Grade-1 recall
      (mean ± SD across three seeds, all three models)
    - Training curves figure

- 3.3 Per-grade performance
    - Table: Precision / Recall / F1 per grade per model
    - Confusion matrix figure (best model)
    - Per-grade F1 bar chart figure

- 3.4 Model stability across seeds
    - Mean ± SD table
    - Best model selection justification

- 3.5 External validation — OAI/Kaggle
    - Table: MRKR test vs OAI/Kaggle performance
    - Performance gap analysis

- 3.6 Subgroup fairness analysis
    - Table: Balanced accuracy by subgroup (best model)
    - Grade-1 recall by subgroup

---

**4. Discussion** (~800 words)

- 4.1 Principal findings summary
- 4.2 Model comparison — which architecture and why
- 4.3 Grade-1 failure — label noise vs architectural limitation
    - Comparison to Zhao et al. 2024 meta-analysis benchmark
- 4.4 Ordinal loss — did it help?
    - Compare to v1 results (standard cross-entropy)
- 4.5 Generalisation — MRKR to OAI/Kaggle
    - DL-inferred vs radiologist labels
    - Domain shift interpretation
- 4.6 Fairness across subgroups
    - Sex, race, weightbearing findings
    - Clinical implications
- 4.7 Limitations
    - DL-inferred labels
    - No knee joint detection
    - ~3-5% flip flag errors
    - 10,000 image subset
    - Single external validation dataset
- 4.8 Future directions
    - Knee joint detection with YOLO
    - 20K dataset expansion
    - Uncertainty quantification
    - Multimodal architecture with clinical metadata

---

**5. Conclusions** (~200 words)
- Key findings
- Clinical implications
- Recommendations for deployment

---

**References**


**Appendices** (do not count toward word limit)
- Appendix A: Filter cascade detailed numbers
- Appendix B: Full per-grade metrics all seeds
- Appendix C: GitHub repository link and README
